title: Imaging Mass Cytometer Tuning
author: Michelle Daniel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bf2gjqbw
materials: Hit “New” to create a new tuning template.

steps.title: If the machine is running, check the “tuning log sheet
steps.instruction: If the machine is running, check the “tuning log sheet.ods” and get the date of the last tuning. If the last tuning was done more than two days ago proceed to step 3.

steps.title: Check argon supply and pressure (5 bar)
steps.instruction: Check argon supply and pressure (5 bar). Make sure that one of the argon tanks has at least 25% which will last overnight. Get a new tank if required from the shop in the basement using the PanGas pay card.

steps.title: Check Helium pressure (2 bar)
steps.instruction: Check Helium pressure (2 bar). Check the Helium bottles in the cabinet. If the pressure drops below 100 bar open the second bottle and close the one that was open. Move the empty bottle in front of the room and hang a sign “Empty” on it. Inform Esther Danenberg or Stefanie Engler to order a new bottle.

steps.title: Open the CyTOF software on the...
steps.instruction: Open the CyTOF software on the computer and log in with your name and password.

steps.title: Insert the tuning slide into the Hyperion
steps.instruction: Insert the tuning slide into the Hyperion.

steps.title: Wait for at least 30 minutes...
steps.instruction: Wait for at least 30 minutes until you proceed with tuning.

steps.title: Confirm that “Adjust E-ref” needs to be performed
steps.instruction: Confirm that “Adjust E-ref” needs to be performed. If not, proceed to tuning.

steps.title: In the camera window move to...
steps.instruction: In the camera window move to an empty region next to an ablated region (right side).

steps.title: Hit “Get Current Coordinate” in the Manual Tune Settings
steps.instruction: Hit “Get Current Coordinate” in the Manual Tune Settings.

steps.title: Copy the values of the Ablation...
steps.instruction: Copy the values of the Ablation Energy Range to the tuning log sheet.

steps.title: Copy the new values of the...
steps.instruction: Copy the new values of the Ablation Energy Range to the tuning log sheet. This process needs to be done every 2-3 weeks. Therein the laser energy is adjusted to guarantee optimal ablation with respect to decreasing laser power output over time. Check the tuning “tuning log sheet.ods” on the IMC computer for the last time this was performed.

steps.title: In the camera window move to...
steps.instruction: In the camera window move to an empty space next to previously ablated regions (right side).

steps.title: Hit “Get Current Coordinate” in the Auto Tune window
steps.instruction: Hit “Get Current Coordinate” in the Auto Tune window.

steps.title: Under Parameters check “QC Report”, “HTI...
steps.instruction: Under Parameters check “QC Report”, “HTI Current/Gases Calibration” and “Transient Calibration”.

steps.title: If tuning does not pass repeat...
steps.instruction: If tuning does not pass repeat one more time with the same parameters.

steps.title: If tuning does not pass again...
steps.instruction: If tuning does not pass again also check “Coarse XY Optimization” and “Fine XY Optimization”.

steps.title: If tuning fails again contact Daniel...
steps.instruction: If tuning fails again contact Daniel Schulz or Esther Danenberg or Stefanie Engler. XY optimization does only need to be performed if the torch box of the machine has been opened or if tuning continuously fails.

steps.title: If tuning passes under Results check the Transient Cross Talk
steps.instruction: If tuning passes under Results check the Transient Cross Talk

steps.title: If this is not the case...
steps.instruction: If this is not the case the Hyperion and the Helios are not well aligned. Align tubing. If you have not obtained training to align tubing contact Esther Danenberg, Stefanie Engler or Daniel Schulz.

steps.title: Check the detector voltage value and...
steps.instruction: Check the detector voltage value and compare with previous tuning results. Large jumps (>50 V) in detector voltage indicate abnormal deterioration of the detector potentially due to sample/gas contaminations. Contact Daniel Schulz.

steps.title: Copy all the parameters required in...
steps.instruction: Copy all the parameters required in the tuning log sheet from the Results to the tuning log sheet on the IMC computer.
